title: SKILL: Password Auditing Tools
note: Goal: develop enough hands-on understanding of Hashcat, John the Ripper, and Hydra to identify UX and workflow failures across all three, then produce a build document for a unified GUI app that does it better.

title: hash type identification
note: Recognize MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, bcrypt, NTLM, LM by structure and length. Use hashid and haiti tools to automate.

title: hashcat attack modes
note: Straight (0), combinator (1), brute-force (3), rule-based (0+rules). Know when CPU vs GPU mode wins.

title: wordlist curation
note: rockyou, SecLists, CeWL for target-specific lists. Understand quality vs size tradeoff. Custom list generation.

title: john the ripper workflow
note: john --format flags, incremental mode, --show output parsing. Single crack mode for fast wins.

title: hydra online attack surface
note: SSH, HTTP-POST, FTP, RDP targets. Understand lockout thresholds, rate limiting, and form parameter parsing.

title: rule-based mutations
note: hashcat -r rules, best64.rule, dive.rule. Writing custom rules for target-specific patterns.

title: GPU vs CPU tuning
note: Workload profiles (-w 1-4), --optimized-kernel-enable, benchmark runs with hashcat -b.

title: hash extraction methods
note: samdump2 for Windows SAM, /etc/shadow for Linux, Mimikatz NTLM dump concepts.

title: rainbow table concepts
note: Pre-computed chains, time-memory tradeoff, why salting defeats them. Ophcrack as reference tool.

title: encoding and format issues
note: Hex vs binary hash input, charset flags, UTF-8 vs ASCII wordlists, line ending problems.

title: output reading
note: Can you distinguish hash format mismatch from exhausted wordlist from wrong mode? Parse status lines correctly.

title: attack failure diagnosis
note: Wrong mode vs wrong hash type vs list exhausted vs bad encoding. Systematic elimination.

title: hashcat example hashes reference
note: hashcat.net/wiki/example_hashes - bookmark and use. Cross-reference format codes with real samples.

title: speed benchmarking baseline
note: Run hashcat -b before sessions. Know your baseline H/s so you can detect configuration problems.

title: john pot file review
note: ~/.john/john.pot tracks cracked hashes. Review after each run to confirm results are valid.

title: hydra output validation
note: Confirm true positives by manually testing returned credentials. False positives happen with HTTP forms.

title: SecLists repository
note: github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists - primary wordlist and payload reference. Know the directory structure.

title: lab environment isolation
note: Need dedicated VM or air-gapped test machine. Never target live systems you do not own.

title: wordlist pre-staging
note: Download rockyou.txt, SecLists subset before sessions. Avoid mid-session bandwidth dependency.

title: GPU driver validation
note: Confirm CUDA or OpenCL working on TINKERSWORKSHOP. Run hashcat -b as first session step.

title: legal scope definition
note: Document that all practice targets are owned or explicitly authorized. CTF platforms count.

title: test hash generation
note: Create known-answer hashes with python hashlib or openssl for controlled practice targets.

title: hydra test server setup
note: Spin up SSH on a local VM or use a dedicated CTF box. Document the target config.

title: distraction blocking
note: Schedule focused blocks. Cracking sessions require attention during setup and diagnosis phases.

title: session 1: hashcat baseline
note: Run hashcat -b. Crack 10 MD5 and 10 SHA1 sample hashes with rockyou. Document mode flags and speed.

title: session 2: rule mutations
note: Apply best64.rule and dive.rule to same hash sets. Compare hit rate vs straight dictionary. Document delta.

title: session 3: john the ripper
note: Same hash sets through JtR. Compare speed, UX, output format, and format flag syntax. Note workflow friction.

title: session 4: format edge cases
note: Test bcrypt, NTLM, SHA-512-crypt. Document where each tool struggles and why.

title: session 5: hydra SSH target
note: Stand up test SSH server on local VM. Run hydra against it. Document lockout behavior and tuning flags.

title: session 6: hydra HTTP form
note: Set up a basic login page. Parse form parameters with hydra. Compare to Burp Suite form analysis.

title: session 7: tool comparison matrix
note: Build a structured comparison: speed, UX, format support, online vs offline, documentation quality.

title: session 8: GUI build document sprint
note: Using pain points from sessions 1-7, write the build document for the unified GUI app. Define features, layout, and UX decisions.
